hero.title: Generate polished product images
hero.description:
Describe what you want to sell, choose the look, background, and lighting, then generate ecommerce product visuals with a much more commercial-photography feel.
Built for storefronts, marketplaces, PDPs, and paid ads.
hero.avatars_tip: Made for ecommerce sellers, operators, designers, and lean brand teams

entry_panel.title: Try a product direction first
entry_panel.description: Choose a product, style, and background here. We will carry those selections into the full generator workspace.
entry_panel.placeholder: For example: sunscreen spray, coffee beans, bluetooth earbuds, scented candle
entry_panel.submit_label: Open the full product image workspace
bullets: Style, background, camera angle, and lighting presets help teams test directions faster
The full workspace adds richer controls like product details, brand color, and composition
The landing page already starts the selection flow instead of acting like a static promo screen
entry_panel.note: This is a lightweight entry panel. Actual generation and download happen in the full product image workspace.

logos.title: Built for a complete product-image workflow

introduce.title: This is not a generic image toy. It is shaped around ecommerce product imagery.
introduce.label: Positioning
introduce.description: Instead of forcing users to think in technical language, the product turns the biggest commercial image decisions into clear controls.

items.title: Centered on what you sell, not on model jargon
items.description: The workflow starts with the product itself, then moves through style, background, lighting, and composition decisions that actually matter for sales visuals.

items.title: Closer to real commercial image direction
items.description: The system leans into product accuracy, material realism, clean edges, believable shadows, and polished commercial retouching.

items.title: Useful across the ecommerce content stack
items.description: Use the output for homepage hero visuals, product detail pages, marketplace thumbnails, ad creatives, and promotional landing pages.

items.title: Fast first step, deeper full workspace after
items.description: The first screen is intentionally interactive, so users can set direction immediately and continue with richer controls inside the main generator.

benefits.title: Why this flow works better for product imagery
benefits.label: Advantages
benefits.description: The hard part of ecommerce image generation is not making something pretty. It is producing visuals that feel commercially usable and repeatable. The landing-to-workspace flow is built around that reality.

items.title: Presets map to selling contexts
items.description: Instead of vague artistic filters, the presets reflect common business directions like clean white-background main images, luxury ad visuals, natural lifestyle scenes, and tech-launch aesthetics.

items.title: Background and lighting shape conversion feel
items.description: The same product can feel cheap, premium, clinical, or lifestyle-led depending on scene and light. That is why those controls are part of the core path.

items.title: The first screen is part of the workflow
items.description: Users should not bounce between marketing copy and product utility. The first screen already starts the journey into the main workflow.

usage.title: Three steps to your first sellable product visual
usage.description: The path from the landing page to the full generator is intentionally compact and repeatable:

items.title: Enter the product you want to sell
items.description: Start with the actual object: a serum, earbuds, coffee beans, skincare jar, kitchen tool, or pet-care product.

items.title: Choose the commercial visual direction
items.description: Pick the style, background, and ratio on the first screen, then refine with brand color, camera angle, lighting, and product details inside the workspace.

items.title: Generate and compare usable outputs
items.description: Review what works best as a main product image, a paid ad visual, or a stronger PDP support image.

items.title: Iterate until the brand feel lands
items.description: If the first image is off, switch the background, angle, or lighting instead of starting over blindly.

features.title: What the current core workflow lets users control
features.description: The interface exposes the choices that matter most for ecommerce visuals, without making users wrestle with raw model settings first.

items.title: Product input
items.description: Every generation starts with what the user is actually trying to sell, which anchors composition and scene language.

items.title: Style presets
items.description: Quickly switch between clean ecommerce, luxury advertising, natural lifestyle, tech-launch, beauty, and food-oriented looks.

items.title: Background presets
items.description: White seamless, gradients, stone surfaces, botanical scenes, kitchens, vanity setups, and acrylic stages are available as ready directions.

items.title: Camera and composition
items.description: Front product shots, three-quarter angles, flat lays, floating packshots, and macro detail views help adapt the story to the product.

items.title: Lighting control
items.description: Softbox, daylight, dramatic spotlight, glowing beauty light, and neutral catalog light all change the final commercial feel.

items.title: Ratio and quality
items.description: Choose square, portrait, and wider layouts with different quality levels for channel-specific output needs.

items.title: One-click product image generation
items.description: The generation flow returns downloadable results for rapid visual testing.

stats.title: Teams prefer a faster product-image workflow
stats.description: because it feels clearer and more commercially usable.

testimonials.title: What users say about the workflow
testimonials.description: Representative feedback around speed, clarity, and output direction.

items.name: David Chen

items.name: Rachel Kim

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about the AI product image workflow
faq.description: These are the practical questions most teams ask before using it in a real ecommerce pipeline.

items.question: Who is this feature best suited for?
items.answer: It is best suited for ecommerce sellers, brand operators, designers, growth teams, and lean founders who need product visuals quickly without depending on a full studio shoot every time.

items.question: How is this different from a generic text-to-image tool?
items.answer: The workflow and presets are tuned for ecommerce product photography use cases. The goal is not just visual novelty, but commercially usable images with stronger product realism and selling intent.

items.question: What is the difference between the hero panel and the full workspace?
items.answer: The hero panel is a fast entry point that sets direction. The full workspace is where users refine product details, brand color, lighting, composition, output ratio, and then run the actual generation task.

items.question: Can it handle both white-background packshots and moodier ad visuals?
items.answer: Yes. The current setup supports clean white-background product shots as well as more premium, lifestyle, or campaign-style scenes through style and background presets.

items.question: Where can the generated images be used?
items.answer: They can be used for storefront hero sections, product detail pages, marketplace main images, promotional landing pages, and ad creatives. Different ratios make it easier to adapt outputs by channel.

items.question: What should users do if the first result is not right?
items.answer: The best move is usually to iterate on the controllable inputs: style, background, lighting, camera angle, and brand color. The product is built to make that iteration loop fast and intentional.

cta.title: Start from the landing page and make your first product image
cta.description: If you already know what you want to sell, open the full workspace now and turn direction into a finished visual.
